title: Resolve (some) compiler warnings
body:
_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_
The test-compile.yml action ends up with a lot of warnings, e.g. [latest main run](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/actions/runs/10406437503) has 47 warnings.  Many of these warnings are simple things like uninitialized/unused variables and deprecated system functions.

_Explain how this is achieved._
Assign default values to variables, add `ifdef` guards to debug variables, replace sprintf with snprintf, and replace `realloc` in the minisat library with `reallocarray` when using GCC.

_If applicable, please suggest to reviewers how they can test the change._
This should be passing all tests (and with fewer warnings), but there might be some weird edge case compilations that I'm not aware of
